danger_signals.visual_form: Robotic enemies in background with mechanical silhouettes
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat; player must shoot to eliminate

danger_signals.visual_form: Bullet trajectories visible in foreground space
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion via jump/dive/roll

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated explosive objects arcing toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Area-of-effect threat; can be shot back or dodged

danger_signals.visual_form: On-screen warning text near player character
danger_signals.meaning: Critical dodge window indicator; bullet evasion required immediately

danger_signals.visual_form: Scaled-up robotic or human antagonist in background
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-culminating threat with distinct attack patterns

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing/highlighted boxes containing gold, silver, or bombs
safety_signals.meaning: Reward for successful engagement; score increase or tactical advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual variants of player weapons (shotgun, machine gun visual states)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary power increase; enhanced damage or fire rate capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinctive high-powered weapon visual with invincibility aura
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary invincibility state; complete damage immunity during gauge depletion

safety_signals.visual_form: Character movement away from projectile paths

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Implied through stage boss design and enemy robot customization
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robots and henchmen deployed across six levels; boss encounters vary by stage
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage progression shows increasing mechanical sophistication and Kid Family presence

faction_indicators.faction: Protagonist Alliance (Annie + Clint bounty hunter partnership)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Character clothing 'emblematic of American frontier period'; distinct silhouettes for Annie vs. Clint
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Foreground positioning; player-controlled character design emphasizes period-appropriate Western aesthetic

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Wild West setting with steampunk/sci-fi overlay
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage environments blend Western landscape elements with mechanical robotic enemies and advanced weaponry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy types shift from biological-inspired to purely mechanical; technology level increases across level progression

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active conflict (story: revenge mission against Kid Family)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Continuous enemy deployment; stage bosses represent escalating Kid Family opposition; background shows stages of confrontation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy aggression patterns increase; boss encounters present distinct attack behaviors unique to each stage

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: D-pad controls player movement; gun reticle holstered
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player capable of jump, dive, roll evasion; full mobility for defensive positioning

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: D-pad controls reticle position instead of character movement; gun extended visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player immobilized in position; movement trade-off for targeting accuracy; shooting mechanics active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinctive visual weapon appearance; invincibility indicator (likely glow or aura effect)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player immune to damage; visual gauge depletes over time indicating power duration

environmental_storytelling.example: Six levels with two stages each plus mini-boss and final boss structure mirrors escalating confrontation with Kid Family; environmental complexity increases

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy robot design reflects world state
environmental_storytelling.example: Mechanical enemy silhouettes in background communicate steampunk/sci-fi contamination of Wild West setting; visual anachronism reinforces narrative conflict

environmental_storytelling.technique: Weapon availability as tactical world communication
environmental_storytelling.example: Shotguns and machine guns appear as stage-specific drops; weapon progression implies increasing enemy sophistication and player resource escalation
Mirage effects and screen shake after major explosions (RoboCop 3 influence) communicate environmental trauma and hazard persistence
Annie and Clint clothed in frontier-period appropriate attire despite sci-fi setting; visual design emphasizes human resistance within mechanized world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Spawn in background; fire projectiles toward player; positioned at varying depths to create aiming complexity
Arc explosive projectiles; projectiles can be shot down or caught (lasso stun) and redirected back to source
Unique attack patterns per boss; escalated projectile volume; represent culmination of stage threat
Reveal item drops (gold, silver, bombs, weapon upgrades); visual loot boxes indicate reward availability
'Look Out!' text appears before critical dodge windows; player must perform jump/dive/roll to avoid projectiles
D-pad shifts from movement control to reticle positioning; player trades mobility for aiming precision; fire button engages shooting

notes: Wild Guns uses layered depth (foreground player positioning vs. background enemy placement) as primary visual communication tool. The 'Look Out!' text bubble was explicitly added to compensate for difficulty judging bullet distances due to artificial 3D depth perception. Visual design intentionally blends Western frontier aesthetic with mechanical/sci-fi elements to communicate narrative conflict (human revenge against mechanized Kid Family). No faction color-coding or symbolic markers documented; factions differentiated primarily through character design (human protagonists) vs. robotic enemy silhouettes. World state communicated through gameplay mechanic shifts (movement vs. aiming control) rather than environmental transformation. Weapon visual variants serve as power-level communication without narrative diegetic explanation.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - Wild Guns article)
